2606.09701.title: Learning to Attack and Defend: Adaptive Red Teaming of Language Models via GRPO
2606.09701.paper_summary: AI red teaming must continually adapt to evolving attackers and defenders. Reinforcement learning offers a promising approach to discovering novel attacks, and co-training methods can produce more robust defenders in tandem. Recent works have demonstrated the efficacy of attacker-defender co-training by applying PPO and DPO, but report that GRPO is unstable in this setting. We introduce AdvGRPO, a co-training framework that makes GRPO viable for joint attacker-defender optimization using dense multi-channel rewards and decoupled advantage normalization. Training progresses through a curriculum from single-turn to closed-loop multi-turn attacks before bootstrapping co-training, where attacker and defender models are updated in alternation. We show that our method can produce highly effective and transferable attacks and that co-trained defenders outperform baselines on safety benchmarks.
2606.09701.paper_summary_zh: AI 紅隊必須不斷適應不斷演變的攻擊者和防禦者。<lb/>強化學習提供了一種有前景的方法來發現新穎的攻擊，而共同訓練方法則可以同時產生更強健的防禦者。<lb/>最近的研究已經證明了攻擊者-防禦者共同訓練的有效性，通過應用 PPO 和 DPO，但報告指出 GRPO 在這種設置中不穩定。<lb/>我們介紹了 AdvGRPO，一個共同訓練框架，使 GRPO 能夠通過使用密集的多通道獎勵和解耦的優勢正規化來實現攻擊者-防禦者的聯合優化。<lb/>訓練通過從單回合到閉環多回合攻擊的課程進行，然後再啟動共同訓練，在此過程中攻擊者和防禦者模型交替更新。<lb/>我們展示了我們的方法可以產生高效且可轉移的攻擊，並且共同訓練的防禦者在安全基準上超越了基線。
2606.09701.author: Blake Bullwinkel et.al.
2606.09701.authors: Blake Bullwinkel, Eugenia Kim, Amanda Minnich, Mark Russinovich
